input.prompt:
My MSTest project has a .testsettings file that configures test deployment, timeout,
and a code coverage data collector. After upgrading MSTest packages, the settings
seem to be ignored. How do I migrate this configuration?

data.content:

seem to be ignored. How do I migrate this configuration?

data.reasoningText: The user has an MSTest project with a `.testsettings` file and wants to migrate to the modern configuration format after upgrading MSTest packages. Let me look at their files to understand the current setup.

arguments.file_text:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<RunSettings>
  <RunConfiguration>
    <!-- Timeout per test in milliseconds (was TestTimeout in .testsettings) -->
    <TestSessionTimeout>30000</TestSessionTimeout>
  </RunConfiguration>

  <MSTest>
    <!-- Replaces AssemblyResolution / deployment behaviour -->
    <DeploymentEnabled>true</DeploymentEnabled>
  </MSTest>

  <DataCollectionRunSettings>
    <DataCollectors>
      <!-- Code-coverage collector (was in AgentRule/DataCollectors) -->
      <DataCollector friendlyName="Code Coverage"
                     uri="datacollector://Microsoft/CodeCoverage/2.0"
                     assemblyQualifiedName="Microsoft.VisualStudio.TraceCollector.SingleFileCodeCoverageDataCollector, Microsoft.VisualStudio.TraceCollector, Culture=neutral, PublicKeyToken=b03f5f7f11d50a3a">
        <Configuration>
          <CodeCoverage />
        </Configuration>
      </DataCollector>
    </DataCollectors>
  </DataCollectionRunSettings>
</RunSettings>

data.reasoningText:
The user has a `.testsettings` file (legacy format) and has upgraded to MSTest 3.8.0. The `.testsettings` format is no longer supported by the modern MSTest runner. They need to migrate to a `.runsettings` file.

Let me create a `.runsettings` file that captures the equivalent configuration:

1. **Test timeout (30000ms)** → `<TestTimeout>` in `<MSTest>` section or `<Timeout>` attribute
2. **Code coverage data collector** → `<DataCollectors>` in `<DataCollectionRunSettings>`


3. **Assembly resolution and deployment** → `<DeploymentEnabled>` in `<MSTest>` section

Now I'll create the `.runsettings` file and update the `.csproj` to reference it.

arguments.file_text:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<RunSettings>
  <RunConfiguration>
    <!-- Timeout per test in milliseconds (was TestTimeout in .testsettings) -->
    <TestSessionTimeout>30000</TestSessionTimeout>
  </RunConfiguration>

  <MSTest>
    <!-- Replaces AssemblyResolution / deployment behaviour -->
    <DeploymentEnabled>true</DeploymentEnabled>
  </MSTest>

  <DataCollectionRunSettings>
    <DataCollectors>
      <!-- Code-coverage collector (was in AgentRule/DataCollectors) -->
      <DataCollector friendlyName="Code Coverage"
                     uri="datacollector://Microsoft/CodeCoverage/2.0"
                     assemblyQualifiedName="Microsoft.VisualStudio.TraceCollector.SingleFileCodeCoverageDataCollector, Microsoft.VisualStudio.TraceCollector, Culture=neutral, PublicKeyToken=b03f5f7f11d50a3a">
        <Configuration>
          <CodeCoverage />
        </Configuration>
      </DataCollector>
    </DataCollectors>
  </DataCollectionRunSettings>
</RunSettings>

data.content: Now wire the `.runsettings` file into the project:

Here's what was migrated from `local.testsettings` → `local.runsettings`:

| `.testsettings` setting | `.runsettings` equivalent |
|---|---|
| `<Property name="TestTimeout" value="30000" />` | `<TestSessionTimeout>30000</TestSessionTimeout>` |
| `<AssemblyResolution>` / deployment | `<DeploymentEnabled>true</DeploymentEnabled>` |
| Code Coverage `DataCollector` (v1 URI) | Code Coverage `DataCollector` (v2 URI) |

**Key points:**

- `.testsettings` is a legacy format ignored by the modern MSTest runner (v3+). The replacement is `.runsettings`.
- The `.csproj` now sets `<RunSettingsFilePath>` so `dotnet test` picks it up automatically (you can also pass `--settings local.runsettings`).
- Once verified, you can safely delete `local.testsettings`.
